draft.introduction: The valley of dry bones vision, Ezekiel’s most famous prophecy. God commands the bones to live, and the Spirit breathes life into a vast army—a vision of national resurrection that shaped later belief in bodily resurrection.

translation.text: The hand of the Lord was upon me, and he brought me out in the Spirit of the Lord and set me down in the middle of the valley; it was full of bones. He said to me, “Son of man, can these bones live?” And I answered, “O Lord God, you know.” He said, “Prophesy over these bones: O dry bones, hear the word of the Lord.”
translation.original_text: καὶ ἐγένετο ἐπ᾽ ἐμὲ χεὶρ κυρίου

translation.text: So I prophesied as I was commanded. And as I prophesied, there was a sound, and behold, a rattling, and the bones came together, bone to its bone. And I looked, and behold, sinews were on them, and flesh had come upon them. And I will put my Spirit within you, and you shall live, and I will place you in your own land.

translator_notes.note: The Hebrew ruach and Greek πνεῦμα both mean “spirit,” “wind,” and “breath” simultaneously. Ezekiel exploits all three meanings: the wind blows, breath enters the bodies, and the Spirit of God animates them. This triple wordplay is untranslatable.
